text: Your brain loves evidence that agrees with you—and quietly ignores the rest. This is called confirmation bias, a cognitive trap we all fall into. Imagine you’re reading reviews for a new phone. You love brand X. You only focus on the glowing reviews and ignore any negative ones. This skews your perception, making you think the phone is flawless. Next time, challenge your views. Seek opposing evidence!
